critic_raw:
Preprocessing is explicitly fit on the full dataset before splitting: 'Pixel values are normalized to [0, 1] using statistics (mean, std) computed on the entire dataset before splitting.' The same is stated for baseline features: 'standardized using statistics fit on the entire dataset before splitting.' This is data leakage in both pipelines. For a ResNet-50 with ImageNet pretraining, the normalization shift from full-dataset vs. training-only statistics may be small. For logistic regression on 180 handcrafted features—some of which have skewed distributions like Haralick values—the statistical leakage is more consequential.

The split is stratified by (hospital site, class label) using random sampling. Multi-site pathology datasets frequently contain multiple images from the same patient's biopsy (serial sections, multiple fields of view). Random splitting without patient-level holdout places slices from the same biopsy in both training and test. The model can learn patient-specific staining artifacts or tissue characteristics rather than cancer morphology, inflating sensitivity estimates.

The validation strategy re-trains both models on combined training + validation (9,500 images) for final evaluation. The CNN is trained with early stopping based on validation sensitivity; this means the validation set is used both as a stopping criterion during training and as a data source for final retraining. The stopping epoch chosen on 7,000 images may overfit on the additional 1,500 validation images during retraining, since the architecture was optimized for the smaller training set's dynamics.

Model selection uses sensitivity as the criterion (5-fold CV for logistic regression; early stopping on validation sensitivity for CNN). Sensitivity alone is a weak selection criterion in imbalanced data: a model that predicts all examples as positive achieves 100% sensitivity, so sensitivity without a paired specificity constraint can select pathological models. The design adds specificity only as a secondary metric without it being enforced during selection.
all_issues_raised: Normalization statistics for both CNN pixel normalization and baseline feature standardization are computed on the full dataset before splitting, constituting data leakage that systematically influences learned representations.

Stratified random splitting by site and class label without patient-level holdout allows multiple slides from the same biopsy to appear in both training and test, enabling patient-identity leakage that inflates sensitivity.

Validation data is used both for early stopping during CNN training and as additional training data in the final retraining step; the stopping epoch was optimized on 7,000 images but retraining proceeds on 9,500 images, violating the independence assumption behind the final evaluation.

Sensitivity is used as the sole model selection criterion without a paired specificity constraint; a model predicting all positives maximizes sensitivity, making sensitivity alone an invalid selection criterion for imbalanced data.
all_issues_adjudicated: Normalization statistics for both CNN pixel normalization and baseline feature standardization are computed on the full dataset before splitting, constituting data leakage that systematically influences learned representations.

Stratified random splitting by site and class label without patient-level holdout allows multiple slides from the same biopsy to appear in both training and test, enabling patient-identity leakage that inflates sensitivity.

Validation data is used both for early stopping during CNN training and as additional training data in the final retraining step; the stopping epoch was optimized on 7,000 images but retraining proceeds on 9,500 images, violating the independence assumption behind the final evaluation.

Sensitivity is used as the sole model selection criterion without a paired specificity constraint; a model predicting all positives maximizes sensitivity, making sensitivity alone an invalid selection criterion for imbalanced data.
